channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Report shows the threat of supply chain vulnerabilities from third-party products
item.description: CyCognito report reveals critical vulnerabilities in web servers, cryptographic protocols, and PII-handling interfaces.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Before Apple releases a television, it needs to fix its TV app, and more
item.description: Earlier this month, the tech sphere was buzzing following a report that Apple was “evaluating” the idea of releasing a television.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: If your Garmin watch faces keep crashing, you're not alone – and Garmin is working on it
item.description: Garmin users have been seeing regular crashes on their watches for months, thanks to faulty firmware.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Two years of ChatGPT: the conversation that never ends
item.description: Two years can be an eternity for the technology industry: plenty of time for enterprises to innovate, launch a new product, peak on the stock market… and then plummet again. Think about the bubbles that briefly surrounded 3D printing, smart glasses, or the metaverse.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who had bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Python 3.14 is a rational constant
item.description: This (half-) month in Python and elsewhere: Get your feet wet with Pi-thon, er, Python 3.14, in its alpha 2 release! See how standalone Python app executables work with PyInstaller. Use Python’s pattern matching to unlock type-based decision-making superpowers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How Anthropic’s new protocol could quickly extend AI’s reach
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: Home for the holidays? Show relatives you care with some tech support
item.description: As the holiday season kicks off, the journey home could also mean that those who are more tech-savvy will be called on to provide a backlog of tech support to parents, grandparents and other family.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Chen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The outlook is uncertain for AI regulations as the US government pivots to full Republican control
item.description: The federal government is about to transition from one that prioritized safeguards for artificial intelligence to one more focused on eliminating red tape.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica And Ali Swenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cloud providers turn to custom chips to meet high demand
item.description: Squeezed between AI-driven demand for ever-faster computing, and shortages of the GPUs used to accelerate those workloads, hyperscalers are designing custom silicon for specific workloads to further improve performance while cutting costs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon’s VP explains how to protect yourself from holiday impersonation scams
item.description: Amazon becomes a prime target of scammers during the holiday shopping season trying to steal Social Security numbers, bank information and Amazon credentials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Copilot Studio Agents get a major upgrade via Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 put an end to Microsoft’s dalliance with chatbots. Not because the company is walking away from natural language interfaces, but because it’s taking what it learned with its first Copilots and refocusing on tangible productivity gains from large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American Airlines is expanding its airport ‘gate lice’ crackdown
item.description: American Airlines is cracking down on “gate lice” ahead of what’s expected to be a record-setting Thanksgiving travel season. The company is expanding new technology to more than 100 airports in hopes of keeping passengers from attempting to board a flight before their assigned group is called.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: How Biden’s AI tech could play a role in Trump’s immigration crackdown
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration—among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to he
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Sharing is not always caring: 7 dangerous things to post online
item.description: Sharing information online like your vacation plans and relationship status can make you a target for scammers; here's what to avoid posting to keep yourself safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson to host Musk, Ramaswamy at Capitol ahead of DOGE work
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will host Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-leads of the newly established "Department of Government Efficiency" (DOGE), at the Capitol on Dec. 5, he announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Fox host: Podcasters like Rogan have 'earned' seats in White House briefing room
item.description: Fox News host Will Cain said popular podcasters like Joe Rogan have “earned” seats in the White House briefing room. When discussing the potential for a shake-up in the James S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Amazon's $4B bet on an AI startup
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Windows Recall: All your privacy questions answered
item.description: Windows Recall is an AI-based feature that will be built into new Copilot+ PCs. Recall takes snapshots of your screen every few seconds and uses that data to make a searchable index of everything you’ve ever done on your PC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Judge won’t alter Google antitrust trial dates to accommodate Trump DOJ’s proposal
item.description: A US federal judge indicated that the trial addressing the Department of Justice’s proposals to curb Google’s dominance in online search will proceed as scheduled in April 2025, even if DOJ officials under President-elect Donald Trump seek to revise the remedies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kotlin to lose scripting features
item.description: JetBrains plans to drop some scripting-related technologies from the Kotlin language, including REPL functionality, an obsolete API for Java scripting, the KotlinScriptMojo Maven plugin, and the kotlin-scripting-ide-services library for implementing code completion functionality.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Equinix to cut 3% of staff amidst the greatest demand for data center infrastructure ever
item.description: Even as demand for data infrastructure surges to an all-time high, Equinix is planning to lay off 3% of its workforce, suggesting a growing skills mismatch in the industry.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Majority of social media influencers share information without verifying its accuracy: UN report
item.description: The majority of social media influencers share information with their followers without verifying its accuracy, according to a new U.N. report that was released on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This free time-zone conversion tool blows other clock apps out of the water
item.description: You’d think by this point in our lives, we’d have the whole time-zone-conversion thing figured out. It isn’t all that complicated, right?! And yet—well, trying to keep such calculations straight often feels like performing advanced algebra.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: 10 ways AI can make farmers’ jobs easier
item.description: For those who work the earth, AI can be an invaluable tool for making that work more efficient, sustainable and productive. AI helps farmers use the same tools they have been using for centuries, but with improved efficiency and focus.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic introduces the Model Context Protocol
item.description: Anthropic today released a new open source protocol to let all AI systems, not just its own, connect with data sources via a standard interface.
item.dc:creator: Info World
